name.context: a holistic and context-based understanding of sustainable buildings

country.context: in a Danish context the differentiation has been between ‘urban ecology’ and ‘environmental regulation’ as two fundamentally different ways of perceiving sustainable buildings

name.context: have had energy savings as their main theme since 1982

country.context: This illustrates that new types of regulation, governance, network management, etc. are typically taking place in parallel with the traditional top-down regulation, or as governance in the shadows of the hierarchy

design_strategies.name: Green wall system
design_strategies.anchor_text: Green walls are a common feature in sustainable buildings.

design_strategies.name: Rainwater harvesting systems
design_strategies.anchor_text: The building uses rainwater harvesting systems to collect and store rainwater for non-potable purposes.

design_strategies.name: Greywater treatment
design_strategies.anchor_text: The building uses greywater treatment systems to reuse water for irrigation and flushing toilets.

ecosystem_services.name: Fresh air
ecosystem_services.anchor_text: assimilating and transforming emissions or contributing chemicals to regulate the concentrations of gases in the Earth's atmosphere

ecosystem_services.name: Soil
ecosystem_services.anchor_text: soil formation and retention of soil associated with ensuring ongoing soil fertility through nutrient cycling and storage, rock weathering, and microbial activity

ecosystem_services.name: Habitat
ecosystem_services.anchor_text: providing access to food/nutrition, place for nursery of young organisms, relevant to both permanent and transient populations
